rulings.Case Title: TOTAL OUTDOOR CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANTs POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, KEVIN HICKS, CHRIS GIOUZELIS, CATHY GIOUZELIS, PETER PRONGOS, PANAGIOTA PRONGOS, RAYMOND REUDY and ADVERTISING DISPLAY SYSTEMS 1, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED pursuant to 425.17(c). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TOTAL OUTDOOR CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANTs POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, KEVIN HICKS, CHRIS GIOUZELIS, CATHY GIOUZELIS, PETER PRONGOS, PANAGIOTA PRONGOS, RAYMOND REUDY and ADVERTISING DISPLAY SYSTEMS 1, LLC's Motion To Set Aside, or in the alternative renewed Motion For Summary Judgment Defendant's Motion to Set Aside, or in the alternative renewed motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Kernan's multiple declarations do not support existence of a triable issue of fact regarding permission to use the roof especially in light of Peter Progos admissions. Moving party had an opportunity to respond to Mr. Kernan's second declaration (February 6, 2014) at the hearing. Other than raising an objection to this rebuttal declaration, Plaintiff did not seeks any relief, such as, for example, continuance for additional discovery. Objections to Mr. Kernan's third declaration (August 27, 2014) are sustained. Renewed motion for Summary judgment as to trespass is denied. Moving party failed to sustained burned under CCP 1008(b). No new facts, circumstances or law presented. Mr. Kernan's third declaration and alleged lack of valid permit do not constitute new facts or circumstances. 1936 case is not new law. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL E. JARA VS. MARIA ELISA PALMA et al

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY HANSON VS. JEFFREY APPENRODT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained as follows: Demurer to the fourth cause of action for intentional infection of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Demurer to the first and eight causes of action for violation of SFRO and fraud are sustained with leave to amend to allege each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendant or to sufficiently allege conspiracy liability. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY HANSON VS. JEFFREY APPENRODT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Paragraph 50 is stricken without leave to amend. As a general rule damages for mental suffering are not available in contract causes of action. Saywer v. Bank of America (1978) 83 Cal.App.3d 135. Plainitff cites no cases to support an exception from the general rule under the circumstances of this case. Paragraph 55 is stricken with leave to amend to sufficiently allege in good faith conspiracy liability. = (501/REQ)
